words.definitionEn: a set of television or radio programmes or podcasts that deal with the same subject or that have the same characters
words.exampleEn: The first episode of the new series is on Saturday.

words.definitionEn: bad or dangerous
words.exampleEn: a serious illness/offence/crime

words.definitionEn: to give somebody food or drink, for example at a restaurant or during a meal
words.exampleEn: serve (something), Breakfast is served between 7 and 10 a.m.

words.definitionEn: a system that provides something that the public needs, organized by the government or a private company
words.exampleEn: the ambulance/bus/postal service

words.definitionEn: more than two but not very many
words.exampleEn: Several letters arrived this morning.

words.definitionEn: more than two but not very many
words.exampleEn: Several letters arrived this morning.

words.definitionEn: to move or make somebody/something move with short quick movements from side to side or up and down
words.exampleEn: The whole house shakes when a train goes past.

words.definitionEn: used with I and we for talking about or predicting the future
words.exampleEn: This time next week I shall be in Scotland.

words.definitionEn: the form of the outer edges or surfaces of something; an example of something that has a particular form
words.exampleEn: a rectangular/spherical/cylindrical shape

words.definitionEn: a large piece of thin cloth used on a bed to lie on or lie under
words.exampleEn: Have you changed the sheets (= put clean sheets on the bed)?

words.definitionEn: a large boat that carries people or goods by sea
words.exampleEn: The yard builds ships for the Navy.

words.definitionEn: a seed, or pod containing seeds, of a climbing plant, eaten as a vegetable. There are several types of bean and the plants that they grow on are also called beans.
words.exampleEn: green beans

words.definitionEn: either of the two parts of the body between the top of each arm and the neck
words.exampleEn: your left/right shoulder

words.definitionEn: a heavy wild animal with thick fur and sharp claws (= pointed parts on the ends of its feet). There are many types of bear.
words.exampleEn: a black bear

words.definitionEn: a loud cry of anger, fear, excitement, etc.
words.exampleEn: angry shouts

words.definitionEn: to say something in a loud voice; to speak loudly/angrily to somebody
words.exampleEn: Stop shouting and listen!

words.definitionEn: not open
words.exampleEn: The door was shut.

words.definitionEn: to close something; to become closed
words.exampleEn: The window won't shut.

words.definitionEn: either of the two halves of a surface, an object or an area that is divided by an imaginary central line
words.exampleEn: the right side of the brain

words.definitionEn: an event, an action, a fact, etc. that shows that something exists, is happening or may happen in the future
words.exampleEn: Looking back, I should have seen the signs.

words.definitionEn: to write your name on a document, letter, etc. to show that you have written it, that you agree with what it says, or that it is real
words.exampleEn: Sign here, please.

words.definitionEn: shiny grey-white in colour
words.exampleEn: a silver car

words.definitionEn: a chemical element. Silver is a shiny, grey-white precious metal used for making coins, jewellery, beautiful objects, etc.
words.exampleEn: a silver ring/chain

words.definitionEn: not complicated; easy to understand or do
words.exampleEn: a simple solution/explanation/question/task/example

words.definitionEn: to defeat somebody in a game or competition
words.exampleEn: beat somebody at something, He beat me at chess.

words.definitionEn: from an event in the past until a later past event, or until now
words.exampleEn: Cath hasn't phoned since she went to Berlin.

words.definitionEn: from a time in the past until a later past time, or until now
words.exampleEn: She's been off work since Tuesday.

words.definitionEn: the activity of making musical sounds with your voice
words.exampleEn: the beautiful singing of birds

words.definitionEn: only one
words.exampleEn: He sent her a single red rose.

words.definitionEn: a ticket that allows you to travel to a place but not back again
words.exampleEn: How much is a single to York?

words.definitionEn: used as a polite way of addressing a man whose name you do not know, for example in a shop or restaurant
words.exampleEn: Good morning, sir. Can I help you?

words.definitionEn: a place where a building, town, etc. was, is or will be located
words.exampleEn: the site of a sixteenth-century abbey

words.definitionEn: how large or small a person or thing is
words.exampleEn: population/sample/group/class size

words.definitionEn: connected with the sport of skiing
words.exampleEn: a ski resort

words.definitionEn: one of a pair of long narrow pieces of wood, metal or plastic that you attach to boots so that you can move smoothly over snow
words.exampleEn: a pair of skis

words.definitionEn: to move over snow on skis, especially as a sport
words.exampleEn: Our children want to learn to ski.

words.definitionEn: the sport or activity of moving over snow on skis
words.exampleEn: He also enjoys skiing and mountain biking.

words.definitionEn: the layer of tissue that covers the body
words.exampleEn: to have dark/pale skin

words.definitionEn: the space above the earth that you can see when you look up, where clouds and the sun, moon and stars appear
words.exampleEn: The sky suddenly went dark and it started to rain.

words.definitionEn: the natural state of rest in which your eyes are closed, your body is not active, and your mind is not conscious
words.exampleEn: I need to get some sleep.

words.definitionEn: at a slow speed; not quickly
words.exampleEn: to move/walk/turn slowly

words.definitionEn: a mobile phone that also has some of the functions of a computer, for example the facility to use apps and the internet
words.exampleEn: You can use your smartphone to access the internet.

words.definitionEn: the quality of something that people and animals sense through their noses
words.exampleEn: a sweet/pleasant smell

words.definitionEn: to have a particular smell
words.exampleEn: + adj., The room smelt damp.

words.definitionEn: the expression that you have on your face when you are happy or you think something is funny, in which the corners of your mouth turn upwards
words.exampleEn: a broad/warm/bright/slight smile

words.definitionEn: to make a smile appear on your face
words.exampleEn: to smile slightly/broadly

words.definitionEn: the grey, white or black gas that is produced by something burning
words.exampleEn: cigarette/tobacco smoke

words.definitionEn: to take smoke from a cigarette, pipe, etc. into your mouth and let it out again
words.exampleEn: Do you mind if I smoke?

words.definitionEn: the activity or habit of smoking cigarettes, etc.
words.exampleEn: No Smoking (= for example, on a notice)

words.definitionEn: a substance that you use with water for washing your body
words.exampleEn: Wash the affected area with soap and water.

words.definitionEn: a game played by two teams of 11 players, using a round ball which players kick up and down the playing field. Teams try to kick the ball into the other team’s goal.
words.exampleEn: I played soccer when I was younger.

words.definitionEn: connected with activities in which people meet each other for pleasure
words.exampleEn: She has a busy social life.

words.definitionEn: people in general, living together in communities
words.exampleEn: She believes that the arts benefit society as a whole.

words.definitionEn: a piece of clothing that is worn over the foot, ankle and lower part of the leg, especially inside a shoe
words.exampleEn: a pair of socks

words.definitionEn: changing shape easily when pressed; not stiff or hard
words.exampleEn: soft margarine

words.definitionEn: meat that comes from a cow
words.exampleEn: roast beef

words.definitionEn: a member of an army, especially one who is not an officer
words.exampleEn: They visited the graves of US soldiers killed in the First World War.

words.definitionEn: a way of solving a problem or dealing with a difficult situation
words.exampleEn: Attempts to find a solution have failed.

words.definitionEn: to find a way of dealing with a problem or difficult situation
words.exampleEn: Attempts are being made to solve the problem of waste disposal.

words.definitionEn: at an earlier time; in the past; already
words.exampleEn: You should have told me so before.

words.definitionEn: in, at or to a place that you do not know or do not mention by name
words.exampleEn: I read somewhere that she refuses to do interviews.

words.definitionEn: a place that you do not know or do not mention by name
words.exampleEn: We need to find somewhere to live.

words.definitionEn: earlier than the time when
words.exampleEn: Do it before you forget.

words.definitionEn: a group or type of people or things that are similar in a particular way
words.exampleEn: sort of somebody/something, ‘What sort of music do you like?’ ‘Oh, all sorts.’

words.definitionEn: a place, person or thing that you get something from
words.exampleEn: renewable energy sources

words.definitionEn: a person who gives a talk or makes a speech
words.exampleEn: He was a guest speaker at the conference.

words.definitionEn: connected with one particular thing only
words.exampleEn: specific needs/requirements

words.definitionEn: a formal talk that a person gives to an audience
words.exampleEn: Several people made speeches at the wedding.

words.definitionEn: the rate at which somebody/something moves or travels
words.exampleEn: at … speed, at high/low/full/top speed

words.definitionEn: a small creature with eight thin legs. Many spiders spin webs to catch insects for food.
words.exampleEn: a poisonous spider

words.definitionEn: to do things in a particular way
words.exampleEn: The doctor behaved very unprofessionally.

words.definitionEn: a tool that has a handle with a shallow bowl at the end, used for mixing, serving and eating food
words.exampleEn: a plastic/metal spoon

words.definitionEn: the way that somebody behaves, especially towards other people
words.exampleEn: good/bad behaviour

words.definitionEn: having four straight equal sides and four angles of 90°
words.exampleEn: a square room

words.definitionEn: a shape with four straight sides of equal length and four angles of 90°; a piece of something that has this shape
words.exampleEn: First break the chocolate into squares.

words.definitionEn: a period or state that something/somebody passes through while developing or making progress
words.exampleEn: at… stage, I can't make a decision at this stage.

words.definitionEn: a set of steps built between two floors inside a building
words.exampleEn: We had to carry the piano up three flights of stairs.

words.definitionEn: a small piece of paper with a design on it that you buy and stick on an envelope or a package before you post it
words.exampleEn: a 67p stamp

words.definitionEn: to have one of the main parts in a film, play, etc.
words.exampleEn: star in something, He starred in that movie about the crazy professor.

words.definitionEn: the point at which something begins
words.exampleEn: If we don't hurry, we'll miss the start.

words.definitionEn: a country considered as an organized political community controlled by one government
words.exampleEn: the Baltic States

words.definitionEn: a period of staying; a visit
words.exampleEn: I enjoyed my stay in Prague.

words.definitionEn: to take something from a person, shop, etc. without permission and without intending to return it or pay for it
words.exampleEn: I'll report you to the police if I catch you stealing again.

words.definitionEn: one of a series of things that you do in order to achieve something
words.exampleEn: We are taking steps to prevent pollution.

words.definitionEn: to be in the right or suitable place
words.exampleEn: Where do these plates belong (= where are they kept)?

words.definitionEn: the organ inside the body where food goes when you eat it
words.exampleEn: stomach pains/cramps

words.definitionEn: a hard solid mineral substance that is found in the ground, often used for building
words.exampleEn: Most of the houses are built of stone.

words.definitionEn: a large shop that sells many different types of goods
words.exampleEn: It's available at London's three biggest stores.

words.definitionEn: very bad weather with strong winds and rain, and often thunder and lightning
words.exampleEn: fierce/heavy/severe/violent storms

words.definitionEn: without a bend or curve; going in one direction only
words.exampleEn: a straight road

words.definitionEn: not in a curve or at an angle; in a straight line
words.exampleEn: Keep straight on for two miles.

words.definitionEn: unusual or surprising, especially in a way that is difficult to understand
words.exampleEn: A strange thing happened this morning.

words.definitionEn: a plan that is intended to achieve a particular purpose
words.exampleEn: the government’s economic strategy

words.definitionEn: pressure or worry caused by problems in somebody’s life or by having too much to do
words.exampleEn: emotional/mental stress

words.definitionEn: to give extra force to a word or syllable when saying it
words.exampleEn: You stress the first syllable in ‘happiness’.

words.definitionEn: a long narrow piece of leather, cloth, etc. that you wear around the middle part of your body
words.exampleEn: to do up/fasten/tighten a belt

words.definitionEn: the way in which the parts of something are connected together, arranged or organized; a particular arrangement of parts
words.exampleEn: the structure of the building

words.definitionEn: showing a lack of thought or good judgement
words.exampleEn: a stupid mistake/question/idea

words.definitionEn: to achieve something that you have been trying to do or get; to have the result or effect that was intended
words.exampleEn: Our plan succeeded.

words.definitionEn: achieving your aims or what was intended
words.exampleEn: The experiment was entirely successful.

words.definitionEn: used to emphasize the great degree of something
words.exampleEn: This issue was of such importance that we could not afford to ignore it.

words.definitionEn: used to emphasize the great degree of something
words.exampleEn: This issue was of such importance that we could not afford to ignore it.

words.definitionEn: quickly and unexpectedly
words.exampleEn: I suddenly realized what I had to do.

words.definitionEn: to put forward an idea or a plan for other people to think about
words.exampleEn: suggest something, I'd like to suggest a different explanation for the company's decline.

words.definitionEn: an idea or a plan that you mention for somebody else to think about
words.exampleEn: Can I make a suggestion?

words.definitionEn: a set of clothes made of the same cloth, including a jacket and trousers or a skirt
words.exampleEn: She was wearing a grey business suit.

words.definitionEn: an advantage that something gives you; a helpful and useful effect that something has
words.exampleEn: Freedom of information brings great benefits.

words.definitionEn: approval that you give to somebody/something because you want them to be successful
words.exampleEn: support for something, There is strong public support for the policy.

words.definitionEn: to help or encourage somebody/something by saying or showing that you agree with them/it
words.exampleEn: support somebody/something, to support an idea/a view

words.definitionEn: to think or believe that something is true or possible (based on the knowledge that you have)
words.exampleEn: Getting a visa isn't as simple as you might suppose.

words.definitionEn: used to say ‘yes’ to somebody
words.exampleEn: ‘Will you get me my coat?’ ‘Sure, where is it?’

words.definitionEn: an event, a piece of news, etc. that is unexpected or that happens suddenly
words.exampleEn: What a nice surprise!

words.definitionEn: to make somebody feel surprised
words.exampleEn: surprise somebody, It wouldn't surprise me if they got married soon.

words.definitionEn: feeling or showing surprise
words.exampleEn: a surprised look

words.definitionEn: causing surprise
words.exampleEn: It’s not surprising (that) they lost.

words.definitionEn: an investigation of the opinions, behaviour, etc. of a particular group of people, which is usually done by asking them questions
words.exampleEn: A recent survey showed 75 per cent of those questioned were in favour of the plan.

words.definitionEn: containing, or tasting as if it contains, a lot of sugar
words.exampleEn: a cup of hot sweet tea

words.definitionEn: a small piece of sweet food, usually made with sugar and/or chocolate and eaten between meals
words.exampleEn: a packet of sweets

words.definitionEn: a person, an object, an event, etc. that represents a more general quality or situation
words.exampleEn: The vase is decorated with religious symbols.

words.definitionEn: an organized set of ideas or theories or a particular way of doing something
words.exampleEn: reform of the country's education system

words.definitionEn: a small computer that is easy to carry, with a large touch screen and usually without a physical keyboard
words.exampleEn: The company has launched its latest 10-inch tablet.

words.definitionEn: most; to the greatest extent
words.exampleEn: Which one do you like best?

words.definitionEn: a speech or lecture on a particular subject
words.exampleEn: I went to several interesting talks at the conference.

words.definitionEn: the most excellent thing or person
words.exampleEn: We all want the best for our children.

words.definitionEn: a result that you try to achieve
words.exampleEn: to meet/hit a target

words.definitionEn: a piece of work that somebody has to do, especially a hard or unpleasant one
words.exampleEn: to accomplish/perform/undertake/complete a task

words.definitionEn: the particular quality that different foods and drinks have that allows you to recognize them when you put them in your mouth
words.exampleEn: a sweet/salty/bitter/sour taste

words.definitionEn: to have a particular taste
words.exampleEn: + adj., to taste good/delicious/sweet

words.definitionEn: the work of a teacher
words.exampleEn: She wants to go into teaching (= make it a career).

words.definitionEn: scientific knowledge used in practical ways in industry, for example in designing new machines
words.exampleEn: science and technology

words.definitionEn: between 13 and 19 years old; connected with people of this age
words.exampleEn: a teenage girl/boy

words.definitionEn: the measurement in degrees of how hot or cold a thing or place is
words.exampleEn: high/low temperatures

words.definitionEn: a word or phrase used as the name of something, especially one connected with a particular type of language
words.exampleEn: a technical/legal/generic term

words.definitionEn: in a more excellent or pleasant way; not as badly
words.exampleEn: She sings much better than I do.
